checks.finding: design.md defines the architecture sequence, component responsibilities and technologies, dependency direction, trust boundaries, isolation state transitions, deployment model, compatibility constraints, and rollback path needed to implement REQ-001 through REQ-011.

design.md Architecture explicitly directs dependencies from orchestrators and review gates to deterministic selectors and validators, then to contracts and policies; selectors and validators never invoke agents or gates, preventing a circular dependency.

design.md Data Plan identifies all new data entities, affected existing artifacts, historical-report compatibility, atomic publication, and the pinned rollback inventory; it also correctly states that no database or application-data migration is required.

design.md Backend Plan, Manifest Contract, and API / Contract Plan define the repository-local CLI surfaces, versioned JSON contracts, required manifest fields, closed-schema behavior, success output, stable diagnostic categories, and compatibility impact; no network API, RPC, or event surface exists.

design.md Security Boundaries and security-spec.md cover forged risk, traversal, symlink escape, TOCTOU, unauthorized inputs and outputs, stale evidence, session leakage, strict parsing, hash verification, atomic publication, human-only terminal resume, and transactional rollback integrity.
The feature type is library, and design.md, frontend-spec.md, and ux-spec.md explicitly state that no browser UI, frontend state, or client/backend interaction is introduced.

design.md Test Strategy names unit, integration, parity, isolation-policy, template, rollback, and regression commands and maps them to TEST-001 through TEST-006; acceptance-tests.md provides concrete fixtures and negative cases for routing, identity reuse, tampering, cost provenance, release synchronization, and rollback atomicity.

Every architecture element used by design.md is defined in its Components table or subsequent contract, routing, isolation, data, deployment, and rollback sections; responsibilities and implementation technologies are specified without an unowned critical component.
design.md Architecture Decision Records identifies accepted ADR docs/adr/0003-turn-first-agent-routing.md for the central routing-order and context-isolation decisions.
